summary: Small single-story rectangular building with a raised stone foundation, stone-brick walls with a row of horizontal windows, and a stepped dark-red (netherbrick) curved/gabled roof capped by light stone corner blocks. A low cobblestone perimeter curb/patio surrounds the base and the structure sits centered in a dirt/tilled pad.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangle with symmetrical long-axis roof; base set into a shallow cobblestone border
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along long axis (front/back mirror)

elements: horizontal row of 4-5 windows centered on long walls
cobblestone curb/patio one block high surrounding footprint
stepped netherbrick roof with slabs/blocks forming a curved profile
raised foundation of stonebrick one to two blocks
rebuild_hints: Footprint: rectangle roughly 11×21 blocks (visible top views show a slender long rectangle); use cobblestone 1-block border around it.
Walls: use stonebrick for walls about 3 blocks tall above foundation; place a continuous band of glass windows (4–5 panes) centered horizontally on long sides.
Roof: build a stepped netherbrick roof with multiple 1-block-high tiers following the long axis; add light stone slab/blocks at roof tier ends for highlights.
Foundation: raise the wall base 1 block above the surrounding pad and sit the cobblestone curb one block lower, creating a recessed walkway.
Symmetry: mirror details left/right and front/back; place window spacing evenly.
uncertainties: Exact internal layout and door location are not visible; entrance position is uncertain.
Precise block choices for roof edging pieces (appears to be stone slabs or chiseled stone) uncertain; labeled as stone_slab with moderate confidence.
Exact footprint block count derived from top views is approximate (width/depth integer estimates may vary by ±2).
